company: PT Kubus Mitra Inovasi / RedStorm Pte.Ltd.

findings.title: 2FA Authentication Bypass via Hardcoded Hidden Field
findings.description: Both the researcher login (/researcher/login) and customer login (/customer/login) pages contain a hidden input field 'password_2fa' with a hardcoded value of 'redstorm'. This completely defeats the purpose of two-factor authentication, as any attacker who obtains a user's primary credentials can bypass 2FA by simply submitting this known value.
findings.impact: Complete bypass of two-factor authentication for ALL users on the platform

findings.title: IDOR - Unauthenticated Access to Researcher Profiles via /views/
findings.description: The /views/ endpoint exposes researcher dashboard/profile information without requiring authentication. By accessing /views/{view_id}, an unauthenticated user can view sensitive researcher information including full name, username, join date, rank, and points. The view IDs are predictable and over 100 unique IDs have been identified in the Wayback Machine archive, many returning HTTP 200 (publicly accessible).
findings.impact: Privacy violation, mass researcher data enumeration, potential targeted phishing

findings.title: Origin IP Disclosure via CSP Header + Complete WAF Bypass
findings.description: The Content-Security-Policy header leaked the internal staging server domain 'redstorm-stable.smtapps.net', which resolves to origin IP 103.10.61.120 (DigitalOcean). By accessing the origin server directly, the Cloudflare WAF is completely bypassed. Additionally, all smtapps.net subdomains resolve to the same origin IP, including admin.smtapps.net, api.smtapps.net, staging.smtapps.net, and dev.smtapps.net.
findings.impact: Complete WAF bypass, origin server directly attackable, DDoS protection negated

findings.title: phpinfo.php Exposed on Origin Server
findings.description: The phpinfo.php file is accessible on the origin server at https://103.10.61.120/phpinfo.php (with Host: www.redstorm.io header). This reveals extensive server configuration details including PHP version (7.3.26 - EOL), document root, Xdebug enabled, open_basedir not set, and other sensitive information.
findings.impact: Complete server configuration disclosure, aids further exploitation

findings.title: Laravel/Lumen Debug Mode Enabled in Production
findings.description: The origin server has Laravel/Lumen debug mode enabled in production. All HTTPS endpoints return detailed error pages with stack traces, file paths, and application internals. The error reveals the application name (api-smartchecking), framework (Lumen), user (wgs-dev), and full directory structure.
evidence: Full Symfony profiler CSS loaded in error page
findings.impact: Source code structure disclosure, aids targeted exploitation

findings.title: MongoDB Port Exposed to Internet
findings.description: MongoDB default port 27017 is open and accessible on the origin server (103.10.61.120). While authentication is required, the port exposure allows for brute-force attacks and version fingerprinting (MongoDB 3.6 - EOL).
evidence: HTTP response: 'It looks like you are trying to access MongoDB over HTTP on the native driver port.'
findings.impact: Potential database access through brute force, version-specific exploits

findings.title: CORS Misconfiguration
findings.description: The CORS configuration has multiple issues: inconsistent ACAO headers (with and without trailing slash), allows all CRUD methods, and includes 'access-control-allow-origin' in allowed headers which is itself a misconfiguration.

findings.title: Username Enumeration via Customer Profile Avatar
findings.description: The endpoint /customer/profile/avatar/{username} allows enumeration of customer usernames by observing the HTTP response. Confirmed username: 'andripwn' returns 302 (exists).
findings.impact: Customer username enumeration for targeted attacks
